fault_hypothesis: The `overlaps` function on line 65 uses strict inequalities (`<=` and `>=`) which treat adjacent windows (sharing only an endpoint) as non-overlapping. The failing tests expect adjacent windows to be considered conflicting, so the condition should use strict less-than (`<`) instead.

constraints.intent: Check that the overlaps function correctly identifies adjacent windows as overlapping when they share an endpoint (strict vs non-strict boundary check).

spec.expr: not (slot[1] == candidate[0] or slot[0] == candidate[1]) or not (slot[1] <= candidate[0] or slot[0] >= candidate[1])

constraints.intent: When a slot shares an endpoint with the candidate, the overlaps function should return True (conflict detected), but the current implementation returns False for adjacent windows.

spec.expr: all(not (slot[1] == candidate[0] or slot[0] == candidate[1]) for slot in existing)

constraints.intent: Before building the merged schedule, verify no existing slot is adjacent to the candidate; if any are adjacent, the function should have returned False already.

spec.expr: all(not (merged__2[i][1] == merged__2[j][0]) for i in range(len(merged__2)) for j in range(len(merged__2)) if i != j)

constraints.intent: After normalization, if adjacent slots were incorrectly allowed through, the merged schedule would contain windows sharing endpoints that should have caused rejection.
